title: Update and extend layout tests
description: Update existing assertions in test_slides.py and test_cli.py that check list_layouts() or the slide layouts command output. Add a new test asserting that every entry returned by list_layouts() contains a non-empty string in the name key. Add or update a CLI test asserting that --plain output includes the name alongside the filename.
acceptance_criteria: All pre-existing layout tests pass without modification to production code
New unit test asserts name is a non-empty string for every layout entry returned by list_layouts()
CLI test verifies --plain output contains both filename and name on each line
uv run pytest exits 0 with no failures

handoff_summary.completed: Updated test_cli.py::TestSlideLayoutsCmd::test_plain_output_one_filename_per_line to check ".xml" in l instead of l.endswith(".xml"). Added test_plain_output_includes_filename_and_name asserting each plain line splits into [filename.xml, non-empty-name]. Added test_slides.py::TestListLayouts::test_name_field_is_non_empty_string asserting each dict has a name key with a non-empty string value. All 90 tests pass including the 9 layout-related tests.
handoff_summary.risks: None. No production code was modified.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only test files were modified; no shared state with sibling beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: test_plain_output_one_filename_per_line now checks ".xml" in l (substring) instead of l.endswith(".xml") since the plain format is "{file}  {name}". The new test_plain_output_includes_filename_and_name splits on "  " (two spaces) to separately assert the filename part ends with .xml and the name part is non-empty.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: TestListLayouts now has 6 tests (was 5): all pre-existing tests unchanged, new test_name_field_is_non_empty_string added. TestSlideLayoutsCmd now has 3 tests (was 2): existing test updated, new test_plain_output_includes_filename_and_name added. 90/90 pass.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: layout.name may be an empty string for layouts without a name attribute in the XML. The test uses the standard python-pptx default template (via Presentation()) whose layouts all carry non-empty names, so the assertion is valid for the fixture.

conflict_risks: None — only test files were modified; no shared state with sibling beads.

execution_history.summary: Updated and extended layout tests for list_layouts() name field. Fixed the broken test_plain_output_one_filename_per_line (was asserting l.endswith(".xml") but plain output now formats as "{file}  {name}"). Added test_name_field_is_non_empty_string to test_slides.py and test_plain_output_includes_filename_and_name to test_cli.py. All 90 tests pass.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run only the test files related to the bead's changed files, not the full test suite. Use `pytest` to target individual test files. If unsure which tests are relevant, use the bead's expected_files and touched_files fields as a guide.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes (e.g. import corrections, fixture setup) only when strictly necessary to run the relevant tests. Do not refactor production code under the guise of test enablement.

Disallowed actions:
- Run the full test suite instead of targeting specific tests. Always use `pytest` for targeted runs. Running the full suite wastes time and often exceeds the agent timeout.
- Use `run_in_background` for any Bash commands. All test commands must run synchronously so their output is available before the structured verdict is submitted. Background tasks cause the structured output to be lost, resulting in a failed bead.
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.
